Morgan Asset \& Wealth Management delivers industry\-leading investment management and private banking solutions. Asset Management provides individuals, advisors and institutions with strategies and expertise that span the full spectrum of asset classes through our global network of investment professionals. Wealth Management helps individuals, families and foundations take a more intentional approach to their wealth or finances to better define, focus and realize their goals.

Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.          **Before you apply**  If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. description: **Role Overview**               This role serves as a strategic Financial Business Partner supporting core exchange businesses. You will work closely with product, operations, data, and management teams to translate complex business activities into financial insights, decision frameworks, and scalable planning models. The position emphasizes business\-facing financial analysis, forward\-looking modeling, and end\-to\-end ownership of business unit economics.              **Financial Support and Strategic Analysis:**       The Finance BP is expected to develop a deep understanding of the exchange’s core business modules, including spot trading, wealth management products, quantitative trading, and fiat\-related services, and translate business mechanics into structured financial models that support growth and product optimization. You will partner with business leaders to formulate and track annual budgets, quarterly forecasts, and resource allocation plans, ensuring alignment between strategic objectives and financial constraints.       A key responsibility is analyzing revenue composition, cost drivers, and user lifecycle value (LTV), linking financial outcomes to product design and user behavior. You will participate in financial feasibility assessments for new products, new markets, and major initiatives, evaluating return profiles and risk exposure. In parallel, you will continuously monitor business line profitability and establish business\-unit\-level P\&L models, improving transparency and accountability across teams.              **Cross\-Functional Collaboration and Process Enablement:**       This role requires close collaboration with business, data, marketing, risk, and legal teams, with the goal of embedding the financial perspective early in business decision\-making processes. You will help optimize how finance participates in business approvals, budget distribution, and capital usage, shifting finance from a downstream reviewer to an upstream partner.       You will also contribute to the development of Finance BP analytics systems, including business dashboards and visualization tools, enabling stakeholders to access timely, decision\-relevant financial insights without heavy manual intervention.              **Data\-Driven Insights and Management Decision Support:**       Working alongside data analytics teams, you will produce structured, insight\-driven analyses that connect business actions to financial results. This includes identifying the financial variables behind operational issues and translating them into actionable recommendations for senior management.       A critical focus is integrating financial data with operational metrics such as trading volume, fee rates, and user activity, strengthening forecasting accuracy and improving management’s ability to anticipate performance trends rather than react to outcomes.              **Qualifications:**     * Bachelor’s degree or above in Finance, Accounting, Business Analytics, Mathematics, Economics, or a related field. * 5\+ years of experience in financial analysis or business analysis roles, with at least 2 years in the digital asset industry, internet or fintech companies, or other high\-growth environments. * Demonstrated hands\-on experience in budget management, business modeling, and P\&L analysis, with a strong track record of operating in a Finance Business Partner capacity rather than a purely reporting\-focused role. * Solid understanding of virtual asset trading structures and platform monetization models, including how financial performance is generated across different exchange business lines.            **Others:**     * Candidates based outside Hong Kong(in Asia timezone) are also welcome to apply.

description: **Company Description**  Anglo American is a leading global mining company reimagining the future of mining to improve lives. With a focus on the responsible production of copper, premium iron ore, and crop nutrients—critical resources for decarbonising the global economy and enhancing food security—Anglo American is at the forefront of sustainable innovation. Through its FutureSmart Mining™ approach, the company integrates cutting\-edge technology, digitisation, and sustainability across the entire mining value chain. Guided by its Sustainable Mining Plan, Anglo American is committed to creating thriving communities, protecting the environment, and delivering enduring value for all stakeholders. Headquartered in London, the company operates across five continents and is listed on both the London and Johannesburg stock exchanges.          **Job Description**  We are looking for  **Engineering Summer Interns**   to join our Projects \& Development (P\&D) team, which plays a key role in innovation and sustainability work. We are seeking motivated interns to support us with a variety of work, based in the London office.          **About us:**   P\&D turns Anglo American's strategic intent into real\-world outcomes by shaping opportunities, designing solutions, and delivering capital projects that create long\-term value. We are an integrated team combining project, technical, sustainability, and innovation expertise to lead the development and delivery of some of Anglo American’s most complex and strategic initiatives. We shape, develop, and deliver projects from early\-stage studies through execution and handover. Our work includes internal transformations, brownfield expansions, new ventures, and early innovations that support the company’s future. We work as one integrated team across disciplines, regions, and functions. A consistent delivery model connects our people, systems, and processes and is supported by clear frameworks and governance that enable alignment, readiness, and performance. We work closely with businesses, operators, technical experts, and corporate teams across Anglo American, such as Legal, Finance, Strategy and Sustainability, to ensure alignment, readiness, and effective delivery.           As a Summer Intern in the P\&D team, you will use your skills in research, literature studies, techno\-economic analysis and report writing, and you may have the opportunity to work on the following:          * Investigating technical initiatives and innovations applicable to Anglo American’s projects and developments * Preparing literature studies, technical reports and presentation packs * Assessing the technical feasibility, economic potential, strategic fit, complexity, risk and impact of various initiatives for Anglo American’s projects and developments * Working on an innovation challenge in mining, potentially focusing on designing an AI\-powered method for identifying breakthrough opportunities in a priority area: ore sorting, leaching or tailings management.    This internship offers an excellent opportunity to explore meaningful and diverse work within responsible and sustainable future mining. You will gain valuable hands\-on experience and insight into how technical, innovation, and sustainability considerations are integrated into project development.           If you are curious, motivated, and eager to contribute to sustainable mining, we would be happy to hear from you!          **Qualifications** **We are looking for individuals with:** * A proactive and responsible approach to work * Good communication and teamwork skills * Science or engineering research experience, either academic or industrial * Knowledge of conducting literature studies and basic techno\-economic analysis * Currently in the second year or above of a Bachelor’s degree, or having recently completed a 2:1 Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in an Engineering or STEM‑related field.    This is a paid internship however accommodation is not provided for the duration of the internship.          **Additional Information** **Who we are**  At Anglo American, we’re redefining what it means to mine responsibly. We lead with integrity, care deeply about people and the planet, and constantly push the boundaries of innovation to work safer and smarter. description: At Arup, you'll gain an insight into the role of an in\-house legal team within a global, purpose\-driven organisation. This internship is designed as a learning experience, giving you the opportunity to observe how lawyers support complex projects, manage risk, and work collaboratively with colleagues across the business. You'll learn from experienced legal professionals, develop core professional skills, and build an understanding of how legal advice contributes to work that supports communities and helps shape inclusive, resilient places .           The Opportunity           This 4\-week summer legal internship offers a unique opportunity to gain first\-hand exposure to the legal considerations that underpin major construction, engineering, and infrastructure projects around the world.           As an intern in Arup's in\-house Legal team, you will see how legal advice supports the delivery of highly technical, multidisciplinary projects. The Legal team's involvement spans from early project strategy and procurement through to contract negotiation, risk management, and delivery. You will be embedded in a collaborative professional environment, working alongside solicitors and paralegals who support Arup's engineers, designers, consultants and project teams across a broad range of markets and jurisdictions.           During The Internship, You Will Gain An Insight Into How Legal Services Operate Within a Multi\-disciplinary Global Consultancy, With Practical, Hands\-on Exposure Including          * Assisting solicitors and trainees on legal issues relating to a broad range of engineering, construction, and infrastructure projects, both in the UK and internationally * Experience drafting and reviewing contractual and commercial frameworks used to deliver major projects * Exposure to both contentious and non\-contentious work * Supporting the Legal team on advisory, contractual and dispute\-related matters * Conducting substantive legal research, including analysis of relevant legislation, case law and industry guidance * Insight into risk management, professional liability, and insurance considerations * Understanding how legal advice supports collaboration between engineers, designers, planners, economists and other specialists across the business * Exposure to the adoption and responsible use of AI, including advice on AI\-related risks, mitigation tools, and how the Legal team uses AI to improve efficiencies and ways of working * Gaining a practical understanding of the day\-to\-day role of an in\-house legal team embedded within a global organisation.    We are committed to creating an inclusive and equitable workplace at Arup—one that reflects the communities we serve and values different perspectives and experiences. We actively work to widen access to professional careers and remove barriers to participation, and we particularly encourage applications from individuals whose backgrounds are under ‑ represented, including those from lower socio ‑ economic and ethnic minority backgrounds.           Is this role right for you?          **We Are Seeking The Following Qualifications, Skills And Attributes**  This internship could be a great fit if you are curious about how law operates in practice, and how legal advice supports the delivery of major and complex projects around the world.           You may be early in your legal studies, or exploring your future career options, but you are keen to gain hands\-on insight into the work of an in\-house Legal team within a global, multidisciplinary consultancy.           You do not need prior experience in construction or engineering. Instead, we are looking for interns who are:          * Currently studying towards, or have recently graduated from, a university degree in law. * Curious, motivated, and keen to understand how law supports real\-world projects * Interested in commercial, construction, infrastructure or project\-based legal work * Interested in AI tools and their practical application within an in\-house legal context * Proactive and have a ‘can\-do' attitude and strong willingness to learn * Detail\-oriented with a focus on delivering work to a high standard * Organised, have good inter\-personal and written/verbal communication skills.   **We're Looking For**  Changemakers: A passion for sustainable development and creating a better world is key.           Innovative minds: Critical thinkers who embrace new ideas and challenge the status quo.           Growth seekers: You have a growth mindset, embrace feedback and are always eager to learn and explore new ways of working.           Adaptable adventurers: You're ready to take on any challenge that comes your way.           You should be able to start this opportunity in Summer 2026\.          **Advert closing date: 1st May 2026**  Please note, in the event of high applicant volume, this role may be closed ahead of the advertised closing date. Early application is recommended.          **Location Preferences**  While the role you applied for is based in the location specified in the job advert, we may have other opportunities that align with your profile. To help us assess this, please let us know which Arup office locations you would be interested in working in on your application form          **Duplicate applications**  Please note, candidates are permitted to submit only one application per recruitment cycle. We recommend reviewing all available opportunities and selecting the role and location(s) that best align with your skills and interests before applying.

description: **TITLE: Network Services (NS) Product Intern**      **DEPARTMENT: Product Strategy**      **REPORTING TO: Senior Product Manager**      **OFFICE LOCATION: London, UK**      **ROLE TYPE: Internship, Hybrid (3 days Onsite, 2 Remote)**      **DURATION: 9 Weeks**               IPC is a fintech company that focuses on the human element. With a global presence, we support local markets with our advanced cloud\-based trading communications and managed connectivity solutions.               Through our portfolio of communications and connectivity solutions, we focus on solving business challenges and adapting to regulatory changes in the fast\-paced global financial markets. This enables our clients to maintain consistent market access, a strong competitive advantage, and enhanced operational efficiency.               Join a team that is dedicated to delivering groundbreaking products and making a significant impact on our clients' success.                www.IPC.com                       **Empowering Innovation, Shaping Futures**              **Overview of the Global Internship Program:**       Our 2026 Global Summer Internship Program is designed to provide students and recent graduates with hands\-on experience in their field of study, offering professional growth, mentorship, and valuable networking opportunities.     * **Professional Growth:**   You will gain hands\-on experience in your field of study, which will be invaluable for your professional development. * **Mentorship:**   You will have the opportunity to learn from experienced professionals, gaining insights and guidance that will benefit your career. * **Networking:**   Being part of our organization will allow you to build a network of contacts within the industry, aiding your future career prospects. * **Skill Development:**   You will enhance both technical and interpersonal skills through targeted training sessions.            **What does the internship program entail?**       As a Product Intern – Network Services, you will play an active role in supporting the product lifecycle of IPC’s connectivity and network product solutions. You will collaborate closely with Product Owners, Sales, and relevant internal teams to accelerate customer adoption of the Network Services products.               You may also:              **Product Lifecycle Support (Network Services)**     * Contribute to market analysis for network services portfolio, including connectivity, low latency trading networks, market data, and infrastructure services. * Support the definition of product requirements, technical feature scoping, and roadmap planning for Network Services. * Conduct market and competitor research to support product positioning and future improvements. * Assist in building and maintaining product information, supporting improvements to documentation, processes, and systems.            **Sales Enablement**     * Assist in developing and maintaining product\-focused sales materials, including product overviews, feature summaries, and value proposition documents. * Support the creation and maintenance of internal product briefs to help the sales team understand key capabilities, differentiators, and target use cases. * Translate product features and updates into customer\-facing messaging to support customer adoption.            **Go to Market (GTM)**     * Support GTM deliverables required for Network Services product launches. * Track and help deliver materials such as product briefs, customer FAQs, sales enablement decks, and pricing structures. * Contribute to the preparation of internal processes within Salesforce and other platforms to support product readiness. * Liaise with Marketing, Legal, Security, and Operations to ensure every GTM element is aligned and completed.            **Cross Functional Collaboration \& AI**     * Proactively identify opportunities to leverage AI tools or automation to improve team processes, workflows, or efficiency, and share recommendations with the team.             Work closely with:     * Sales /Sales Engineering * Network Operations / Customer Outcome * Marketing * Exchange Compliance \& Reporting * Legal \& Compliance            **How You Will Make an Impact:**     * **Show Your Enthusiasm:**   Your motivation and eagerness to learn will bring positive energy to our team. * **Take Initiative:**   Don’t be afraid to propose new ideas and solutions. Your fresh perspective can make a real difference. * **Collaborate Actively:**   Work closely with your teammates, sharing insights and supporting each other to achieve common goals. * **Be Proactive:**   Ask questions and seek opportunities to take on new tasks and challenges. * **Demonstrate Commitment:**   Show your dedication to the projects you work on, contributing your best effort. * **Enhance Team Dynamics:**   Your positive attitude and willingness to help will foster a collaborative and supportive work environment. * **Learn and Grow:**   Take advantage of every learning opportunity to develop your skills and knowledge. * **Adapt and Innovate:**   Be open to new ways of working and embrace changes that can lead to improvements.            **Essential Skills and Experience to be Successful in the Role:**     * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in progress. * Advanced English language skills. * Understanding or strong interest in financial industry technologies. * Strong communication and organization skills (leading small meetings, driving objectives, tracking initiatives). * Demonstrated ability to leverage AI tools to support research, content development, analysis, or process improvement. * Exposure to using AI applications and data management techniques to improve information accuracy and workflow efficiency is desirable. * Previous experience supporting a product introduction or market launch is a plus. * Strong analytical skills and ability to work in fast\-paced and multi\-disciplinary environments.            **Curious about what we offer?**     * 9 weeks full\-time paid internship filled with exciting events and initiatives * Access to LinkedIn Learning * Access to IPC University * Improve your technical and soft skills through integrated training and development workshops * Gain invaluable exposure to leadership and the corporate environment * Build lasting connections through networking and relationship\-building opportunities * Connect with your peers through exciting social events            **Additional Information:**       At IPC, we believe that hybrid working creates an inclusive, flexible environment where employees can perform at their best, and teams can collaborate, innovate, and celebrate successes together. We spend around 60% of our time in the office and around 40% of our time working remotely. Some individuals may be required to work from the office or client sites more than 60% of the time, if required by their role and/or client needs.               Your precise work schedule will be determined by you, your Line Manager and HR before commencement of your internship program with IPC.              **IPC’s Work Culture:**       The IPC work culture is one that fosters inclusion, prioritizes innovation, and maximizes potential. We are a global ecosystem, full of diverse people that together made IPC what it is today.               Our strength as an organization is the sum of our different backgrounds, perspectives, skills and geographies; supported by an ironclad commitment to constructive dialogue and open\-mindedness.               We live and breathe our commitment to innovation by embracing bold ideas, seizing new opportunities and striving for excellence. Our people have continued to deliver ground\-breaking solutions to our clients for over 50 years.

We do this through our unparalleled global network. We provide a broad range of financial services and products to our clients – whether they be consumers, corporations, governments or institutions – to help them meet their biggest opportunities and face the world's toughest challenges.          **Shape your Career with Citi**  Citi’s  **Markets**   business provides world\-class solutions and an unmatched global presence. We serve corporates, institutional investors and governments from trading floors in almost 80 countries. The strength of our underwriting, sales and trading and distribution capabilities span asset classes and currencies, providing us with an unmatched ability to meet the needs of our clients.           We’re currently looking for a high caliber professional to join our team as  **Officer, Prime Brokerage Client Service Analyst \- Hybrid**   (Internal Job Title: Securities \& Derivatives Analyst 2 – C10\) based in Hong Kong. Being part of our team means that we’ll provide you with the resources to meet your unique needs, empower you to make healthy decision and manage your financial well\-being to help plan for your future. For instance:          * Citi provides programs and services for your physical and mental well\-being including access to telehealth options, health advocates, confidential counseling and more. That’s why in early 2020 we began rolling out our expanded Paid Parental Leave Policy to include Citi employees around the world. * We empower our employees to manage their financial well\-being and help them plan for the future. * Citi provides access to an array of learning and development resources to help broaden and deepen your skills and knowledge as your career progresses. * We have a variety of programs that help employees balance their work and life, including generous paid time off packages. * We offer our employees resources and tools to volunteer in the communities in which they live and work. description: **This role is for London so those in Europe can apply.**                      **Our Client**       Our subsidiary Global Markets Recruitment (“GMR”) is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on emerging and developed markets at entry to middle\-management level.               We have been asked to asked by our client to run a campaign to identify entry level interns for this summer 2026\. Our client is a bulge bracket bank and one of the pre\-eminent investment banks.               This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices. We will run campaigns for them on an ongoing basis through the first half of this year.               Our client is looking for people in their global offices and we will post these roles for each location. Please apply to the location closest to you.               You will either be placed in their FIG or TMT Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry people also in energy, Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.               The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity\-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.                      **Your Focus**       As an Investment Banking Analyst \- Intern, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.               The role is execution\-driven and detail\-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.               This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high\-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.                      **Responsibilities**     * Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M\&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates * Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options * Prepare client\-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation * Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution * Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines * Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders * Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials * Assess transaction\-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements * Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work * Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams             This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.               You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well\-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.                      **Minimum Qualifications**      **Graduate – Analyst**     * At university studying any degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based including Scientific, Engineering, Arts etc * Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client\-ready materials * High attention to detail and strong organisational skills * Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities * Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations                    **Process**       This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets. Thereafter once selected, you will undergo our client one day assessment centre four interviews and one group role play. A decision will be made on the day.               Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp               Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.                      **Reward Structure**     * You will be well compensated for this time and the intern program will last for six weeks. We are running three programs from mid\-June, end June and mid\-July * Receive a bonus for your time * A permanent offer of employment and part of the Banks’ graduate program * Relocation is provided by the company. Starting salaries £75,000 and up to £90,000 depending on experience * Bonus program of on\-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high\-achievers.

description: **Company Description** **About Revaia**  Revaia is a technology investor based in Paris, London, Berlin and Munich. We partner with the next generation of European digital scale\-ups and help them build leadership positions in a sustainable way.           We are a young, complementary, gender\-equal, and diverse team with entrepreneurial and finance backgrounds. We build bridges between venture, private and public markets.           We are sparring partners for innovative entrepreneurs who are working to transform our world for the better. So far, we have partnered with 20 technology companies: Synera (Industrial AI), Intersec (govtech), Definely (legal AI), Sekoia, (cybersecurity), Ampeco (charge point management solution), FASST (insurance distribution platform), Aircall (a cloud telephony software), GetAccept (a sales enablement software), gohenry (a mobile neobank for kids), Welcome to the Jungle (a digital workplace platform), Yubo (a social discovery mobile application), Frontify (a digital asset management platform), Hublo (a staff management solution for the healthcare sector), Planity (a marketplace for the beauty sector), Algolia (a search and discovery API solution), deepki (an ESG data intelligence platform), Coralogix (an in\-stream observability platform), platform.sh (a next\-generation hosting platform), Kaiko (a crypto market data platform), Mews (a cloud\-based hotel property management system) and Epsor (an employee savings and retirement solution).           We partner with and look for technology companies across a large spectrum of industries and business models. Our typical investment range is €15m to €30m, from series B to post\-IPO investments.          **Job Description**  Based in London, the intern will be part of the investment team and will be supervised by 9 investors in London, Paris, Berlin and Munich. She or he will be fully involved in all steps of the investment process.          **Key contributions include:** **Sourcing:**   in\-depth analysis of targeted sectors or geographies to identify potential new investment opportunities. Participation in industry forums and tech\-related events.          **Assistance in the investment process:**   market review, work with industry experts, financial analysis and modelling, participation in meetings with companies, advisors, and partners, preparation of investment memos for Investment Committee.          **Assistance in the fundraising process:**   preparation and update of presentation materials, sourcing of potential LPs, drafting of follow\-up emails.          **Preparation of weekly meetings:**   deal flow review, new investment opportunities, and presentation of analyses carried out.          **Portfolio support:**   work with and help support the entrepreneurs and teams of our portfolio companies.          **ESG support:**   make sure ESG processes and criteria are integrated all along the investment cycle and promoted among portfolio companies.          **Qualifications** **Profile we are looking for:** * Availability: Can you commit for 6 months starting July 2026? We're looking for full\-time dedication in this timeframe * Experience Requirement: At least one internship experience in finance (VC, PE, M\&A …) or strategy consulting * Mindset: We appreciate a strong entrepreneurial mindset and a keen interest in the technology ecosystem * Skills: Autonomy, multitasking, and full proficiency in English are essential * Work Eligibility: You must be eligible to work in the UK    Additional Information          **Start date:**   July 2026          **Location**   : London          **Duration:**   6 months          **Apply:**   Send the application through SmartRecruiters          ***Revaia will only consider candidates who have the legal right to work in the job’s location and will not provide sponsorship for this role.*** *At REVAIA we are committed to the work of advancing diversity, equity, and inclusion within our organization. We are committed to ensuring to all of our employees and potential candidates equal opportunities, without any consideration of gender, ethnic background, religion, sexual orientation, social status, physical abilities, or age.*

description: **About the Company**                        Apollo Research    is a rapidly growing AI safety and model evaluation company, providing third\-party evaluations for frontier AI models. The capabilities of current AI systems are evolving rapidly. While these advancements offer tremendous opportunities, they also present significant risks, such as the potential for deliberate misuse or the deployment of sophisticated yet misaligned models. At Apollo Research, our primary concern lies with deceptive alignment, a phenomenon where a model appears aligned but is, in fact, misaligned and capable of evading human oversight.               At Apollo, we aim for a culture that emphasizes truth\-seeking, being goal\-oriented, giving and receiving constructive feedback, and being friendly and helpful. If you’re interested in more details about what it’s like working at Apollo, you can find more information    here    .              **About the Role**                       You’ll be Apollo’s second Finance hire. You’ll work with our Senior Finance Manager to execute the day\-to\-day engine that keeps Apollo running and convert business activity into consistent, trusted numbers for management and investors. This role starts part\-time, about 1\.5 to 2 days a week, in person in London. As we double in size over the next 12 months and you show your strengths, your responsibilities and days will increase, with a clear path to a full\-time position. This isn’t a side gig; we’re looking for someone who wants to grow with us. You'd be able to help build a finance team from the ground up.                      **Responsibilities**                     * Run the Procure\-to\-Pay and Order\-to\-Cash cycles * Handle supplier invoices and receipts * Create customer invoices * Prepare supplier payments * Follow up on overdue balances * Ensure every transaction is recorded accurately in Xero * Reconcile bank accounts across our multi\-entity and multi\-currency operations to keep our cash positions accurate every week                **Qualifications**                     * You’re at least part\-qualified (ACA, CIMA, ACCA, AAT) * You understand double\-entry bookkeeping intuitively and can visualize how a transaction flows through the ledger without guidance * You have 2\+ years experience in a transactional processing role managing high\-volume Order\-to\-Cash and Procure\-to\-Pay cycles without letting quality slip                **Required Skills**                     * You’re a wizard with Xero and modern finance platforms like Payhawk, Revolut, Mercury, or similar tools * You use AI tools daily to work faster and smarter * You can pick up new AI tools quickly and know when to use them and, just as importantly, when not to * You are meticulous and spot variances and mistakes * You act with integrity and meet deadlines * You are high\-agency and take initiative to solve problems * You are hungry to develop and want this role to become full\-time                **Preferred Skills**                     * Experience in a fast\-paced startup environment * Strong analytical skills * Ability to work collaboratively in a team                **Pay range and compensation package**                       £220–280 per day as a contractor at first, moving to a pro\-rata salary based on £40–50k FTE in the first 3\-6 months.                      **Equal Opportunity Statement**                       Apollo Research is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We value diversity and are committed to providing equal opportunities to all, regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sex, or sexual orientation. Your Privacy and Fairness in Our Recruitment Process: We are committed to protecting your data, ensuring fairness, and adhering to workplace fairness principles in our recruitment process. To enhance hiring efficiency, we use AI\-powered tools to assist with tasks such as resume screening. These tools are designed and deployed in compliance with internationally recognized AI governance frameworks. Your personal data is handled securely and transparently. We adopt a human\-centred approach: all resumes are screened by a human and final hiring decisions are made by our team. If you have questions about how your data is processed or wish to report concerns about fairness, please contact us at info@apolloresearch.ai.                       Applications deadline: We encourage early submissions and are aiming for a 1 May 2026 start date.

description: An exciting opportunity to lead expense, banking, and treasury operations for a UK‑based life insurance business, covering regulated, service, and holding entities.               This is a high‑visibility role within Finance, offering ownership of core financial processes, responsibility for driving efficiency and automation, and the chance to act as a trusted adviser to senior leadership. You’ll play a key role in ensuring strong financial control, effective decision‑making, and alignment with wider group standards in a regulated insurance environment.               Key Responsibilities     * Lead expense and banking functions across UK entities, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and strong financial controls. * Act as a finance partner to senior stakeholders, providing clear analysis, insight, and constructive challenge. * Own end‑to‑end expense processes, including planning, budgeting, management reporting, and group recharges. * Drive process improvement and automation to improve efficiency and strengthen governance. * Manage liquidity, cash positioning, payroll funding, and collateral obligations. * Oversee bank reconciliations and all bank‑related ledger postings. * Lead intercompany recharging, invoicing, and reconciliations. * Manage employee expense policies and reimbursement, including corporate card schemes. * Act as a subject matter expert for UK purchase order systems. * Support tax processes and statutory audits relating to expense and treasury areas. * Build strong relationships across the wider group and contribute to global initiatives.             Qualifications \& Experience     * Part‑qualified or newly qualified CA, ACA, or ACCA. * **Essential: proven experience within the insurance sector.** * Experience in expense management, purchase ledger, and treasury operations. * Exposure to UK statutory entities and group environments is highly desirable.             What You’ll Bring     * Strong analytical skills with the ability to clearly communicate financial insight. * A delivery‑focused mindset with high ownership and accountability. * A continuous‑improvement approach, with interest in automation and process optimisation. * The confidence to operate in a regulated, matrix‑managed insurance environment. * Experience with ERP finance systems (e.g. SAP) is advantageous.

description: **Responsibilities**  Team Introduction           We are the International LIVE Finance BP — a reliable, diverse, and engaging group of finance professionals. Despite spanning different time zones and locations, we proactively create opportunities to connect. We believe that our foundation of strong collaboration and genuine, relaxed interaction is key to our success.           Join us, and become part of a team that continuously contributes to the inspiring of creativity and the enriching of life.           As a project intern, you will have the opportunity to engage in impactful short\-term projects that provide you with a glimpse of professional real\-world experience. You will gain practical skills through on\-the\-job learning in a fast\-paced work environment and develop a deeper understanding of your career interests.           Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis \- we encourage you to apply early.          **Job Description** * Support daily budget usage review and approval process, ensuring completeness and compliance with internal policies and budget guidelines; * Follow up with business teams and relevant stakeholders to collect supporting documents and resolve finance\-related queries in a timely manner; * Assist in tracking budget consumption and maintaining accurate financial records and working files; * Assist in coordinating with other finance teams for data collection and consolidation; * Provide support for other daily financial operational tasks and ad\-hoc assignments as required.   **Qualifications**  Minimum Qualifications          * Currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree or above, preferably in Finance, Accounting, Economics or related majors; * Basic knowledge of management accounting, internship experience in accounting firms or internet finance/accounting roles is a plus; * Strong sense of responsibility and ownership, with a careful and detail\-oriented working style; * Well\-organized and able to manage routine and repetitive tasks with consistency and accuracy; * Proficient in Microsoft Excel (formulas, data organization, pivot tables preferred); * Reliable, proactive in follow\-ups, and able to meet deadlines; * Available at least 3 days per week for a minimum of 3 months; long\-term availability preferred.    Job Information          **About TikTok**  TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.           Why Join Us           Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day.           We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.           Diversity \& Inclusion           TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

* High standards of integrity, confidentiality, independence and objectivity.

A leading hedge fund is seeking a talented Quantitative Strategist with deep expertise in equity microstructure and algorithmic trading research to join the systematic equities team. You will focus on discovering and developing high\-Sharpe intraday and short\-horizon alpha signals using order book dynamics, market microstructure, and execution data. Your research will directly influence live trading strategies and execution algorithms with significant capital deployment.
 



  





**Responsibilities** 



* Conduct in\-depth microstructure research using tick\-level and order book data to identify predictive signals related to liquidity dynamics, order flow imbalance, adverse selection, queue position, and short\-term price formation.
* Research, design, and optimize intraday and short\-horizon alpha signals for systematic equity strategies and execution algos.
* Develop and enhance algorithmic trading strategies, including passive posting logic, liquidity\-seeking algorithms, optimal order sizing, volume surprise models, and dynamic hedging frameworks.
* Perform rigorous statistical analysis, backtesting, point\-in\-time validation, and transaction cost modeling to ensure signals and algos are robust and scalable.
* Collaborate closely with execution traders, portfolio managers, and developers to productionize research into live trading systems.
* Continuously monitor strategy performance and adapt models to evolving market microstructure conditions.



  





**Requirements** 



* Advanced degree (MSc or PhD) in a quantitative field: Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, Computer Science, Financial Engineering, or related discipline.
* At least 3 years of experience in hands\-on experience in equity microstructure research or algorithmic trading strategy development (sell\-side electronic trading, prop trading, or hedge fund experience strongly preferred).
* Demonstrated ability to extract alpha signals from order book dynamics, tick data, and high\-frequency market data.
* Strong programming skills in C\+\+, C\# or Java.
* Deep understanding of market microstructure concepts.
* Proven track record of improving execution algorithms or developing profitable short\-horizon signals.
* Ability to work independently on research while collaborating effectively in a fast\-paced, performance\-driven environment.

description:
At Warner Music Group, We’re a Global Collective Of Music Makers And Music Lovers, Tech Innovators And Inspired Entrepreneurs, Game\-changing Creatives And Passionate Team Members. Here, We Turn Dreams Into Stardom And Audiences Into Fans. We Are Guided By Three Core Values That Underpin Everything We Do Across All Our Diverse Businesses
   

  

* Curiosity: We do our best work when we’re immersing ourselves in culture and breaking through barriers. Curiosity is the driving force behind creativity and ingenuity. It fuels innovation, and innovation is the key to our future.
* Collaboration: Making music and bringing it to the world is all about the power of originality amplified by teamwork. A great idea, like a great song, travels globally. We ignite passions and build connections across our diverse community of artists, songwriters, partners, and fans.
* Commitment: We pursue excellence for our team and our talent. Everything in music starts with a leap into the unknown, and we’re committed to keeping the faith, acting with integrity, and delivering on our promises.


 WMG is home to a wide range of artists, musicians, and songwriters that fuel our success. That is why we are committed to creating a work environment that actively values, appreciates, and respects everyone. We encourage applications from people with a wide variety of backgrounds and experiences.
   

  

 Consider a career at WMG and get the best of both worlds – an innovative global music company that retains the creative spirit of a nimble independent.
   

  

****Job Title** 
 : Analyst, Business Intelligence \- 12 Month Contract**
**A Little Bit About Our Team**
 WMG's Business Intelligence team (BI) works to influence WMG's strategy and day\-to\-day operations by developing thoughtful insights \& intelligence from its core business practices. Th BI team works with various departments across Warner Music's Recorded Music \& Publishing Divisions to understand the trends and dynamics that drive the constant evolution of our business. Our goal for this role is to develop insights and strategies that support our family of labels and operators in the pursuit of breaking new artists and expanding our reach on a global level. We work with partners across the company to establish best practices for breaking artists and sustaining catalog repertoire across consumption platforms, gauge the impact of marketing initiatives and campaigns, and work to understand user behavior around the world. The teams we work with are located throughout the world with over 40 offices worldwide which does create a certain level of demand and while we always prioritize work life balance, candidates should expect work outside of normal business hours at times.
   

  

**Your Role**
 Intelligence is the lifeblood of today's music business, and in this role you'll get a chance to apply your creativity and problem\-solving skills to help our labels and artists connect with consumers across an array of media and mediums. Because the BI team works closely with departments across the company, you will have the opportunity to see the inner workings and decision\-making within a global music company \- and the ability to influence those decisions with thoughtful intelligence work. We're a collaborative team that loves learning new things, sharing ideas, thinking about the future of the music industry \- and helping our company get there.
   

  

**Here You'll Get To**
* Assist in developing metrics and frameworks for performance measurement across WMG labels, repertoire, artists and songwriters.
* Develop compelling analytics projects and presentations that deliver actionable insights and recommendations to business users.
* Immerse yourself in vast internal and external datasets to conduct trend analysis across consumption, audience, and financial datasets.
* Develop dashboards and frameworks to monitor business and product performance. Work directly with a global network of internal clients and stakeholders.


**About You**
* Experience conducting data analysis across datasets, using database query systems, spreadsheet, and modeling software (e.g., Python, SQL, R, etc.).
* Experience with analytics, BI, data insights, marketing, and strategy, preferably within the music and/or digital media industry
* Excellent time\-management and organizational skills with the ability to work independently and prioritize your workload to meet deadlines.
* Experience with a data visualization tools (e.g. Tableau, Looker, PowerBI)
* Presentation skills with an attention to detail
* Highly independent and self\-motivated
* Comfortable working with internal clients and presenting findings


**We'd Love It If You Also Had**
* Internship or Full time work experience in a music company, record label, or artist management company
* Familiarity with the streaming music landscape or other digital media


 As the home to 10K Projects, Asylum, Atlantic Music Group, East West, FFRR, Fueled by Ramen, Nonesuch, Parlophone, Rhino, Roadrunner, Sire, Warner Records, Warner Classics, and several other of the world’s premier recording labels, Warner Music Group champions emerging artists and global superstars alike. And our renowned publishing company, Warner Chappell Music, represents genre\-spanning songwriters and producers through a catalog of more than one million copyrights worldwide. Warner Music Group is also home to ADA, which supports the independent community, as well as artist services division WMX. In addition, WMG counts film and television storytelling powerhouse Warner Music Entertainment among its many brands.
   

  

 Together, we are
 **Warner Music Group: Independent Minds. Major Sound** 
**.**
**Love this job and want to apply?**
 Click the “Apply” link at the top of the page, or apply directly with your LinkedIn. Applying with LinkedIn will import all of the information you put in your profile, but will still allow you to upload a resume and cover letter.
   

  

 Don’t be discouraged if you don’t hear from us right away. We’re taking our time to review all resumes, and to find the best people for WMG.
   

  

 Thanks for your interest in working for WMG. We love it here, and think you will, too.
   

  

 This position requires a minimum of 4 days per week in the office. We value in\-office collaboration, which is essential for talent development and strong working relationships.
   

  

**WMG is committed to inclusion and diversity in all aspects of our business. We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and will evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, religion or belief, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or gender reassignment, marital or civil partnership status, disability, pregnancy, childbirth or any other characteristic protected by law.**

**About Us** 




 Chaucer is a leading insurance group at Lloyd's, the world's specialist insurance market. We help protect industries around the world from the risks they face. Our customers include major airlines, energy companies, shipping groups, global manufacturers and property groups.
 



  





 Our headquarters are in London, and we have international offices in Copenhagen, Miami, Dubai and Singapore to be closer to our clients across the world. To learn more about us please visit our website www.chaucergroup.com
 



  





**Job Profile Summary** 




 We are seeking an experienced Business Data Analyst with deep expertise in data analysis within the London Market and Commercial \& Specialty Insurance sectors.
 



  





 In this pivotal role, you will help drive Chaucer’s data strategy by shaping and delivering high\-impact data initiatives across the organisation. Acting as a key interface between business stakeholders and data delivery teams, you will ensure that outputs from our data platforms are not only insightful and actionable, but also continuously refined to meet evolving business needs.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 




 Data Analysis \& Requirements Gathering

* Analyse business data to uncover trends, risks, and opportunities that inform strategic and operational decisions.
* Plan and lead requirements\-gathering sessions (e.g. interviews, workshops), translating business needs into clear, actionable requirements and user stories.
* Collaborate with data engineers to ensure solutions are aligned with business objectives and technical feasibility.
* Partner with data engineering teams to optimise data pipelines and support scalable data models.
* Support test teams by converting business requirements into test scenarios and assisting in test execution.
* Monitor progress of data delivery initiatives, ensuring timely updates and effective communication with stakeholders.
* Commit to continuous learning through professional development and skills enhancement.



 Insight Generation \& Reporting
 


* Translate complex data into actionable insights that support business performance and strategic goals.
* Design and maintain intuitive dashboards and reports using Power BI or similar tools, ensuring outputs are accurate, relevant, and tailored to stakeholder needs.
* Work with cloud data platforms to access, transform, and analyse large datasets.



 Stakeholder Engagement
 


* Build strong relationships across Chaucer’s business units to understand reporting needs and deliver fit\-for\-purpose data solutions.
* Present insights and recommendations in a clear, business\-focused manner.
* Provide guidance and support to stakeholders throughout the data delivery lifecycle.



 Data Governance \& Quality
 


* Promote data quality, consistency, and governance across cloud and on\-premise environments.
* Support the development and maintenance of data dictionaries, lineage documentation, and metadata management.



 Teamwork \& Agile Collaboration
 


* Actively contribute to data initiatives and cross\-functional projects.
* Participate in agile ceremonies including daily stand\-ups, backlog grooming, and show \& tell sessions.



  





**Skills and Competencies** 



* Experience as Business Analyst within Commercial and Specialty Insurance markets. Understanding of Lloyd’s market operations, London Market processes, Core Data Record specifications.
* Understanding of data platforms, data transformation, and reporting ecosystems.
* Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to navigate complex business and technical landscapes.
* Demonstrated ability to translate business needs into technical requirements and product outcomes.
* Comfortable working in agile or iterative delivery environments.
* Familiarity with data governance, data quality, and modern data technologies is a plus.



  





**Education** 



* Bachelor’s degree; industry certifications in business / data analysis or insurance domain desirable.



  





**Why Join Chaucer?** 




 Chaucer is a leading global insurer operating in both Lloyd's and company markets, helping industries worldwide manage risk\-from nuclear, shipping, manufacturing, and property. Headquartered in London, with offices in Copenhagen, Bermuda, Sydney, Ireland, Miami, Dubai, and Singapore, we're close to our clients wherever they are.
 



  





 We have shown strong financial success with our Gross Written Premiums growing from $1\.4bn in 2019 to $3\.5bn in 2024\. Backed by strong teams, platforms, and client relationships, Chaucer is poised for continued success.
 



  





**We offer:** 




 A flexible hybrid work model that supports individual and team needs.
 



 A diverse, inclusive culture that values people for who they are.
 



 Extensive, non\-contributory benefits, including medical, life, and pension cover, flexible holidays, and wellbeing support.

**Freelance \| 8–20 hrs/week \| Remote (EU/UK)**
 Are you an experienced data scientist who instinctively spots gaps in analytical reasoning and understands the complex realities of data science in a business context? Do you have 8 to 20 hours per week available alongside your main job or consulting projects? Join us in shaping how AI understands and applies data science expertise at scale.
   

  

**About The Role**
 10x.team connects expert freelancers with leading AI labs building next\-generation models. We are looking for knowledgeable data scientists based in the EU or UK to enhance the accuracy, depth, and real\-world relevance of AI\-powered data science solutions.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Review and refine AI\-generated outputs related to data science, machine learning, statistical modeling, and practical aspects of deploying data\-driven solutions
* Evaluate AI responses for accuracy, application of statistical and machine learning methods, and compliance with real\-world requirements
* Draft realistic data science scenarios based on your direct professional experience
* Create scenario variations from different perspectives (e.g. data scientist, business stakeholder, manager, or client)
* Identify gaps, oversights, or weak reasoning in AI\-generated data science content


**In Simple Terms**
 You will assess and improve AI\-generated content, ensuring it reflects true data science reasoning, authentic documentation, and practical analytical procedures. Rather than conducting traditional data science projects, you’ll apply your expertise to help AI systems understand and reason about data science at scale.
   

  

**Who You Are**
* A senior\-level data scientist with significant professional experience within the EU or UK
* Experienced in advising on, preparing, and executing data science or machine learning projects
* Skilled at evaluating analytical approaches, data quality, and compliance
* Comfortable working independently and providing structured, critical feedback
* Available for 8–20 hours per week, with prompt availability


**Why Join?**
* Flexible, fully remote freelance work that fits your current commitments
* Apply your data science expertise in a rapidly evolving, high\-impact AI environment
* Directly contribute to building advanced AI\-powered data science systems
* Free access to our in\-house AI Academy to further develop your AI skillset
* Clear onboarding, structured tasks, and ongoing opportunities for collaboration


**How To Apply**
 To complete your application, you’ll receive an email right after applying with a link to:
   

  

* A short written evaluation
* A brief AI\-powered interview
* A quick compliance check (credentials \& identity)


**Important:** 
 Your application is only complete once you finish these steps via the link in the email.
   

  

 After successful onboarding, you’ll be eligible to start on upcoming projects as they become available.
   

  

 Join us to help shape the data science expertise of the world’s most advanced AI models!

description:

 WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. With exceptional talent, trusted data and intelligence, and world\-class partnerships – all united by our pioneering agentic marketing platform, WPP Open – we help clients navigate change, capture opportunity, and deliver transformational growth.
   

  

 WPP Media is WPP's AI\-driven media operating unit, bringing together media, data, and partnerships to deliver creative personalisation at scale. Connected through WPP Open and powered by Open Intelligence, clients see exactly where, how, and why their media investment is working.
   

  

 For more information, visit wppmedia.com.
   

  

**2026 Graduate Trainee Programme**
 Reports To: Vice President, Media Delivery and Operations
   

  

 Function: Media Management \& Delivery
   

  

 Location: Hong Kong
   

  

**Role Summary And Impact**
 This comprehensive 18\-month rotational program is designed to build the next generation of industry leaders. As a Graduate Trainee, you will gain hands\-on experience through concentrated rotations across key business functions. This program will provide a deep understanding of the biddable media ecosystem (including Ad\-Operations, Programmatic, Search, Social, and SEO), Data \& Technology, and Research. In addition to media expertise, you will gain valuable leadership exposure by helping to shape our company culture and leading company\-wide events as a member of our Young Committee (Y\-Co), preparing you for a fast\-tracked career as a subject matter expert within WPP Media.
   

  

**Core Responsibilities**
**Rotational Development:** 
 Actively participate in rotations across Biddable Media (Ad\-Operations, Programmatic, Paid Search, Paid Social, SEO) and Data \& Tech (Research) to build specialist expertise.
   

  

**Campaign Management \& Execution:** 
 Drive the end\-to\-end management of advertising campaigns, including setup, execution on major biddable platforms, and performance analysis.
   

  

**Performance Optimization:** 
 Analyze campaign data to identify trends and insights, providing actionable recommendations to improve campaign performance and achieve client objectives.
   

  

**Data Storytelling \& Application:**

**Leadership \& Cultural Contribution:**

 At WPP Media, we believe in the power of our culture and our people. It’s what elevates us to deliver exceptional experiences for both our clients and each other. In this role it will be critical to embrace WPP \& WPP Media’s shared core values:

* An appreciation for data and numbers, with the ability to translate findings into campaign insights.
* Excellent communication skills and a collaborative, team\-oriented mindset.


 Desired Skills \& Experience:
   

  

* Fluent in spoken and written English and Cantonese is a plus.
* Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Advertising, Communications, Business, or a related field.
* Prior internship or work experience in a relevant field.


**Life At WPP Media \& Benefits**

Do you have 8 to 20 hours per week available alongside your main work or consulting projects? Join us in shaping how AI learns, interprets, and applies investment expertise at scale.

 10x.team connects expert freelancers with leading AI labs building next\-generation models. We are looking for experienced investment professionals based in the EU or UK to enhance the precision, depth, and practical realism of AI\-powered investment systems.
   

  

 What You Will Do
   

  

* Review and refine AI\-generated outputs on investment topics such as portfolio construction, asset allocation, due diligence, risk assessment, investment analysis, and market trends

* A senior\-level investment consultant, asset manager, wealth advisor, or financial analyst

* Several years of direct experience in investment analysis, portfolio management, client advisory, or asset allocation

* Comfortable working independently and providing structured, critical feedback

 Accenture is a leading global professional services company, providing a broad range of services in strategy and consulting, interactive, technology and operations, with digital capabilities across all of these services.  **Julie Sweet, Accenture CEO**
**In our team you will learn:**

- The management of complex identity deliveries from conception to sign off

- Enhance your existing skill sets to refine your identity and access management knowledge and define ground breaking technologies

- Architecture Design \& Strategy: Design and implement comprehensive identity and access governance (IAG) strategies.

- Develop and maintain the architecture for identity management, access control, authentication, and authorization solutions.

- Ensure the alignment of IAG solutions with business objectives, security policies, and regulatory requirements.

- Solution Development: Lead the development and deployment of IAG solutions, including role\-based access control (RBAC), attribute\-based access control (ABAC), and privilege management.

- Design workflows for user provisioning, de\-provisioning, access requests, approvals, and certifications.

- Collaborate with software development teams to integrate IAG solutions with existing IT infrastructure and applications.

- Compliance \& Risk Management: Ensure that IAG solutions comply with relevant regulations, such as GDPR, HIPAA, and SOX.

- Conduct risk assessments related to identity and access management and recommend mitigation strategies.

- Implement and maintain audit trails and reporting mechanisms to track access and identity\-related activities.

- Stakeholder Collaboration: Work closely with security, IT, and business teams to gather requirements, design solutions, and ensure smooth implementation.

- Provide technical leadership and guidance to teams involved in the implementation and maintenance of IAG solutions.

- Communicate complex IAG concepts and solutions to non\-technical stakeholders in a clear and understandable manner

**The following skills and experience are essential for this role:**

- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Cybersecurity, or a related field. A Master's degree or relevant certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISM, TOGAF) is a plus.

- 7\+ years of experience in identity and access management (IAM) or security architecture.

- Proven experience in designing and implementing IAG solutions in large\-scale environments.

- Technical Skills:Deep knowledge of identity governance and administration (IGA) tools like SailPoint, Okta, Oracle Identity Governance, or similar.

- Strong understanding of access control models, identity federation, SSO, and MFA technologies.

- Proficiency in security protocols such as SAML, OAuth, OpenID Connect, and LDAP.

- Experience with cloud\-based identity management solutions (e.g., Azure AD, AWS IAM).

**As a Security Consultant, you will:**

- Be client facing to interact closely with the client to help resolve client challenges

- Have a deep knowledge of at least one IAG product

**Set yourself apart:**
- Deep understanding of IGA principles and best practices.

- Strong knowledge of identity and access management (IAM), security frameworks, and regulatory requirements.

- Excellent problem\-solving and analytical skills.

- Strong communication and interpersonal skills.

- Ability to work independently and lead cross\-functional teams.

**What’s In It For You**
 At Accenture in addition to a competitive basic salary, you will also have an extensive benefits package which includes 25 days’ vacation per year, private medical insurance and 3 extra days leave per year for charitable work of your choice!
   

  

**Flexibility and mobility are required to deliver this role as there may be requirements to spend time onsite with our clients and partners to enable delivery of the first\-class services we are known for.**

description:
**Requirements**
**Description \& Requirements**
**Who we are**
 lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and other athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing, connecting, and being well. We owe our success to our innovative product, emphasis on stores, commitment to our people, and the incredible connections we make in every community we're in. As a company, we focus on creating positive change to build a healthier, thriving future. In particular, that includes creating an equitable, inclusive and growth\-focused environment for our people.
   

  

**About This Team**
 The Inventory Management and Sales \& Operations Planning (S\&OP) team delivers inventory strategies that create a premium product experience for every guest, in every market. The team initiates and maintains programs to ensure optimal inventory placement across distribution centres, the existing store fleet and new store openings \& optimizations. We do this to support the strong execution of Merchandise strategies and to deliver exceptional business results.
   

  

 Inventory Management (IM) is responsible for the optimization of full price \& markdown inventory availability across our mainline retail network to drive revenue, reduce costs and increase profitability. Our goal is to match demand with supply wherever the guest chooses to shop by having the right product, in the right place, at the right time.
   

  

 As an Analyst, Planning \& Inventory Management, you are responsible for analysing and overseeing inventory management for a specific portfolio of stores and programs for the APAC region. Your role involves in\-depth analysis to optimize inventory levels in stores considering business needs, sales projections, and store capacity. You will be actively monitoring key metrics on a weekly basis, using data to understand trends, identify shifts in patterns and react accordingly to maximize sales and mitigate potential risks.
   

  

**A day in the life**
* Oversee inventory management for a designated portfolio of stores, focusing on divisional and class\-level performance
* Provide premium inventory management support for a portfolio of high\-volume and brand\-important stores including in\-depth analysis of store inventory levels and performance at the SKU level to identify opportunities and act with custom inventory pushes, inventory parameter adjustments, initial allocation reviews \& adjustments and executing end\-of\-life management strategies
* Sustain circular feedback loops with a select portfolio of high\-volume and brand\-important stores using multiple communication methods (e.g. Teams Channel, meetings, store visits)
* React to inventory escalations and inquires with a sense of urgency, validating issues through analysis and pattern recognition, consulting with relevant partners, providing action/solution, and sharing back learnings to larger group discussion of market trends
* Maintain short\- and medium\- range forecasting efforts at store level, including revisions to inbound/outbound inventory flow strategies
* Apply advanced knowledge of product systems (Anaplan) to manage and maintain quarterly readiness setups, replen and transit calendar management, accurate store configurations, troubleshoot issues and recommend system enhancements
* Generate daily and weekly reports, creating tools and templates as needed for more effective and efficient analysis
* Arrange and present analysis and insights to internal teams and cross\-functional partners
* Execute markdown inventory allocation to support in business driving initiatives i.e. Sale Events
* Handle ad\-hoc tasks and special projects based on evolving business needs


**Qualifications**
* 3\+ years of relevant experience in planning, analytics, forecasting, or inventory management
* Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Data Analysis, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience in a similar role
* Exceptional attention to detail with the ability to design and implement formulas that proactively validate outputs and reports
* Excellent interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills, with the ability to convey complex information clearly
* Proven ability to build strong working relationships and communicate effectively with diverse stakeholders
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with a demonstrated ability to develop innovative solutions to new challenges
* Advanced proficiency in MS Office, including Excel, PowerPoint, Power BI, and related tools
* Collaborative team player with the ability to work independently and drive results
* Self\-motivated problem solver, proactive, and resourceful in identifying and addressing issues
* High sense of urgency with the ability to thrive in an agile, fast\-paced environment


**Must Haves**
* Acknowledges the presence of choice in every moment and takes personal responsibility for their life.
* Possesses an entrepreneurial spirit and continuously innovates to achieve great results.
* Communicates with honesty and kindness, and creates the space for others to do the same.
* Leads with courage, knowing the possibility of greatness is bigger than the fear of failure.
* Fosters connection by putting people first and building trusting relationships.
* Integrates fun and joy as a way of being and working, aka doesn’t take themselves too seriously.


**Beyond The Paycheck (Benefits \& Perks)**
 At lululemon, we care for and invest in the whole person – body, mind, spirit. Our total rewards program is designed to support you in achieving your goals and focus on the things you love. In addition to competitive base pay and a team\-based bonus program, we also offer paid time off, generous employee discounts, fitness/yoga classes, parenthood top up program and personal and professional development programs.
   

  

 Note: availability of these benefits and perks may be subject to your location \& employment type and may have certain eligibility requirements. The Company reserves the right to alter these benefits and perks in whole or in part at any time without advance notice.
